editionData.title: Daily Brief for April 26, 2026: Global Headlines, Markets, Hacker News, Product Hunt
editionData.description: A daily brief for April 26, 2026 covering global headlines, market closes, Hacker News, and Product Hunt.
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Calls Off Witkoff and Kushner’s Travel to Pakistan for Peace Talks
The 85-Year-Old Widow Snagged by Trump’s Immigration Crackdown
headlines.summary: In her first interview since being deported, Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, the French widow of a former G.I., recounted her experience in ICE detention.

headlines.title: The Elite ‘Doctors’ Who Care for Mount Everest
headlines.summary: Glacial ice has sealed the gateway to Everest’s summit, keeping hundreds of climbers from reaching the top. “Icefall doctors” are on the case.

headlines.title: Indigenous Speakers Are Booed During Commemorations of Australia’s War Dead
headlines.summary: Dawn services for Anzac Day were disrupted in three cities. The hecklers seemed to be targeting a widespread Aboriginal custom meant to acknowledge the land’s original inhabitants.

headlines.title: Raqqa Showcases Syria’s Tumultuous Past
headlines.summary: The city on the Euphrates, once a cradle of civilization, suffered under a succession of recent rulers. Our visit found it hoping for change.

headlines.title: Trump safe after shots fired at White House correspondents’ dinner
Trump evacuated from White House correspondents’ dinner after shots fired
Iran says no talks under siege; Trump cancels envoys’ trip to Pakistan
Explosion in southwest Colombia kills at least 14, state governor says
Family longest held in US immigration detention re-arrested after release
Watch: President Trump speaks about shooting incident at correspondents' dinner
headlines.summary: President Trump and several cabinet members were safely rushed from the event in Washington, D.C. after several loud sounds were heard. The Secret Service said one person was in custody.

headlines.title: New CEO Steve O'Donnell vows to unite NASCAR and return the fun
headlines.summary: Steve O'Donnell was introduced as the sanctioning body's chief executive officer at Talladega Superspeedway on Saturday and vowed to "make some moves" that will return the storied racing series to its roots.

headlines.title: Dirk Kempthorne, former Idaho governor and U.S. Interior secretary, dies at 74
headlines.summary: Dirk Kempthorne, a Republican, was elected mayor of Boise at age 34 and served seven years before serving one term in the U.S. Senate and then as governor until 2006.

headlines.title: In a rare interview, a leader of the world's largest right-wing group talks to NPR
headlines.summary: The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.

headlines.title: Rocky Balboa statue takes up a new home inside Philly art museum
headlines.summary: The bronze sculpture is on display inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art as part of a new exhibition on the impact and cultural importance of statues.

headlines.title: EU leaders to address Iran war fallout, mutual defense at Cyprus summit
headlines.summary: AYIA NAPA, Cyprus - European Union (EU) leaders gathering in Cyprus on Thursday and Friday will discuss the economic and security fallout from Iran war, especially on soaring energy prices and mutual defense following a recent drone attack on the Mediterranean island.

headlines.title: SE. Asia seeks domestic fuels amid energy crunch
headlines.summary: Southeast Asian countries are leaning harder on homegrown energy sources — from coal to palm oil and sugarcane — as soaring costs and supply risks make Middle Eastern oil and gas imports less viable.

headlines.title: Red Cross envoy applauds Beijing's humanitarian action
headlines.summary: Balthasar Staehelin, personal envoy of the International Committee of the Red Cross president to China, has lauded China's increasing engagement in international humanitarian action, praising the country's consistent support for respect for international humanitarian law and its diplomatic efforts to promote the peaceful resolution of crises.

headlines.title: Mutual gains via Five-Year Plan welcomed
headlines.summary: China and the US can create a better future for relations, Chinese Ambassador to the United States Xie Feng said on Monday, calling on the nations to seize the opportunities presented by China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-30).

headlines.title: Economies to feel war heat across globe
headlines.summary: The war in the Middle East will likely affect the global economy and the United States, whether it continues for a short or long term, because of disruptions in the oil market that could trigger inflation and spur a recession, warn experts, economists and the International Monetary Fund.

headlines.title: Trump safe after being rushed from White House correspondents dinner, shooter in custody
Taiwan foreign minister arrives in Eswatini after president's trip blocked
Trump says he canceled US negotiators' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks, Fox News reports
Israel says it will attack Hezbollah 'forcefully' as four killed in strikes on southern Lebanon
German government suspects Russia of Signal attack targeting politicians, sources say
